

**Davao Oriental State University**

College of Computing and Information Sciences

ITMSD 2 — Advance Mobile Application Development

| USER MANUAL |
| :---: |

Application Usage Guide

Second Semester, Academic Year 2025–2026

**Group: HABIT HERO**

|   INSTRUCTIONS |
| :---- |
|   The PM coordinates this document. Each Junior Dev writes the section for   their assigned screens. Include screenshots for every major feature.   The goal is that someone unfamiliar with the app can use it after reading this. |

# **1\. App Overview**

**App Name: HabitHero**

HabitHero is a mobile application designed to help users build and maintain positive habits. It allows users to track daily activities, monitor progress through streaks, and stay motivated with inspirational quotes. The app is intended for individuals who want to improve their productivity, health, or daily routines.

# **2\. Installation**

## **2.1 Prerequisites**

* Android device running Android 8.0 (API 26\) or higher  
* OR Android emulator via Android Studio  
* Flutter SDK 3.10.7 or higher installed Dart SDK compatible with Flutter 3.10.7+ Android Studio or VS Code with Flutter and Dart plugins

## **2.2 Setup Steps**

1. Clone the repository: git clone https://github.com/erlyx-12345/HabitHero.git   
2. Navigate to project: cd HabitHero  
3. Install dependencies: flutter pub get  
4. Run the app: flutter run  
5. Ensure a device or emulator is running: flutter devices To build a release APK: flutter build apk –release

# **3\. Feature Walkthrough**

## **3.1 Dashboard Screen**

The Dashboard is the main screen of HabitHero. It displays all your habits for the selected date, along with a date scroll bar at the top and time-of-day filters (All, Morning, Afternoon, Evening). A motivational quote is shown at the top when an internet connection is available.

|   SCREENSHOT — HOME / LIST SCREEN |
| ----- |
| ![Home](../assets/screenshots/homeScreen.png) 
**Step-by-step:** 1\. On launch, the Dashboard shows your name, a progress ring (0%), and today's date highlighted in the date scroll bar at the top. 2\. Tap any date in the scroll bar to view habits for that day. Tap ALL, **MORNING**, **AFTERNOON**, or **EVENING** to filter habits by time of day. 3\. Tap MARK AS DONE on a habit card to log it as complete. Tap \+ NEW in the bottom-right corner to add a new habit. |

## **3.2 Create New Habit**

Use the Create Habit screen to add a new habit to your daily routine. You can choose from preset templates for popular focus areas or create a fully custom habit.

|   SCREENSHOT — CREATE NEW RECORD |
| ----- |
| ![CREATE RECORD](../assets/screenshots/createRecord.png)  
**Step-by-step:** 1\. Tap \+ NEW on the Dashboard. The SELECT FOCUS screen appears showing all available focus areas. Tap a focus area or tap NEW FOCUS to create a custom one. 2\. Select a habit template from the list (e.g., Go sugar-free, Limit caffeine intake) or tap Create Custom Habit at the top to build a fully custom habit. 	 
![CREATE RECORD](../assets/screenshots/customize.png) 
**Step-by-step:** 3\. On the CUSTOMIZE screen, enter a Habit Name, choose an icon, select a color, set a Schedule (Anytime, Morning, Afternoon, Evening), optionally toggle Set a Reminder, set an End Date, then tap CREATE HABIT. |

## **3.3 Habit Details Screen**

The Habit Details screen shows all information about a specific habit including its current streak, best streak, focus area, time of day, and a history of your completions.

|   SCREENSHOT — VIEW RECORD DETAILS |
| ----- |
| ![VIEW RECORD](../assets/screenshots/viewRecord.png)  
**Step-by-step:** 1\. Tap the Streaks icon in the bottom navigation bar. The Streaks screen shows your Current Streak, Habit Finished count, and a list of ALL HABIT STATS for each habit. 2\. Tap any habit in the ALL HABIT STATS list to open its detailed analytics panel showing Longest Streak, Current Streak, Days Missed, Consistency %, and Total Accomplished days. 3\. Tap Done to close the analytics panel and return to the Streaks overview. |

## **3.4 Edit Habit**

You can update any habit's title, focus area, time of day, icon, color, or end date at any time from the Habit Details screen.

|   SCREENSHOT — EDIT RECORD |
| ----- |
| ![EDIT RECORD](../assets/screenshots/editRecord.png) 
**Step-by-step:** 1\. On the Dashboard, tap the three-dot menu (⋯) on any habit card. A bottom sheet appears with options: Edit Configuration, Skip for Today, and End Habit Series. 2\. Tap Edit Configuration. The EDIT HABIT screen opens showing the current Habit Name, Visual Style (icon and color picker), Schedule, and Advanced Settings (reminder time and end date). 3\. Make your changes — update the name, select a new color, change the schedule, adjust the reminder time, or update the end date — then tap UPDATE HABIT to save. |

## **3.5 Delete Habit / Skip Habit**

HabitHero gives you two ways to manage a habit you no longer want to track. Skip for Today hides a habit from the Dashboard for the current day only — it will reappear automatically tomorrow with no impact on your streak. End Habit Series (Retire) permanently stops the habit from today onwards while keeping all your past logs and streak history safe. If you want to fully remove a habit and all its history, use End Habit Series followed by account data management.

|   SCREENSHOT — DELETE HABIT / SKIP HABIT |
| ----- |
| ![DELETE RECORD](../assets/screenshots/deleteRecord.png) 
! [DELETE RECORD](../assets/screenshots/deleteRecord_2.png)    
**Step-by-step:** 1\. On the Dashboard, tap the three-dot menu (⋯) on any habit card. A bottom sheet appears with three options: Edit Configuration, Skip for Today, and End Habit Series. 2\. To skip the habit for today only, tap Skip for Today — the habit disappears from today's Dashboard and will reappear automatically tomorrow with no effect on your streak. To retire the habit permanently, tap End Habit Series — a "Retire Habit?" confirmation dialog appears explaining that the habit stops from today onwards but all past logs and streaks remain safe. 3\. On the "Retire Habit?" dialog, tap STOP FROM TODAY to retire the habit, or tap KEEP TRACKING to cancel and continue tracking it. |

# **4\. Troubleshooting**

| Problem | Possible Cause | Solution |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| App won’t start | Flutter environment not set up correctly or dependencies not installed. | Run flutter doctor to check your setup, then run flutter pub get and try again. |
| Habit not saving | SQLite database may not have initialized correctly. | Uninstall and reinstall the app to reset the local database. |
| Motivational quote not loading | No internet connection available. | The app will display a fallback offline message automatically. Connect to the internet to load live quotes. |

# **5\. FAQ**

**Q: How do I reset the database?**

A: Go to the Profile screen and tap the Delete Account / Reset button. This will clear all user data and return the app to the onboarding screen.

**Q: Can I use the app offline?**

A: Yes. All habit data is stored locally on your device using SQLite. You can create habits, log completions, and view streaks without an internet connection. An internet connection is only needed to load the motivational quote.

**Q: How do I export my data?**

A: The app does not currently support data export. All data is stored locally on the device in the habithero.db SQLite database file.
